occupation: Partner, Media & Entertainment Group Head - Investment Banking at Solomon Partners
headline: Partner, Media & Entertainment Group Head — Investment Banking
summary:
25+ years M&A experience.  Media and Tech investment banker at Solomon Partners.  Former Paul, Weiss M&A attorney admitted to state bars of NY and Mass.  

Former head of Barclays'​ (originally Lehman Brothers) Out-of-Home Advertising, TV Broadcasting and Radio coverage, and senior member of the firm’s Defense/Activist team. Member of Lehman's Global M&A Group and its Global Advisory Committee. While at Lehman founded and sold an ecommerce company.  

Advised on numerous transactions worldwide, including Europe and Asia, and executed over $45 billion in completed transactions. Recognized by digital media industry executives and professional publications as a leading investment banker in the media and tech sectors. 

Frequent industry speaker. 

University guest lecturer providing education in the digital signage, digital out-of-home and interactive technology industries.

Clients have included Adams Outdoor Advertising, AMI Entertainment, Broadsign, Branded Cities, Clear Channel, Cieslok Media, CityBridge, Cox Enterprises, DirecTV, Discovery Communications, Disney, GSTV, Liberty Media, Lionsgate Entertainment, Mediacom Communications (special committee), Netflix, New Tradition Media, OUTFRONT Media, Pacific Outdoor, PatientPoint, RevZilla, RMG Networks, Scripps Networks, TiVo, Valpak and Vector Media.  

Private equity clients have included Acon Investments, Ares, Clairvest, General Atlantic, GI Partners, KKR, LCatterton, MidOcean Partners, Pegasus Capital, Platinum Equity, Providence Equity, Rockbridge Growth Equity, Searchlight Capital and Warburg Pincus.

Author of Times Square Everywhere.  

Author of Digital Sign Language.  

Received joint LL.B and B.C.L degrees in 2000 from the Faculty of Law at McGill University. Prior to law school, studied business at McGill’s Faculty of Management and was named to the Dean’s Honor List. Recipient of the J.W. McConnell Scholarship. 40 Under Forty by NACVA / CTI.  Dealmaker of the Year by Business Worldwide.  Investment Banking MD of the Year, Corporate Vision's Executive Awards.

accomplishment_publications.description: Written by Mark Boidman, a veteran financial advisor in the media and technology sectors, Times Square Everywhere is a top-selling analysis of the seismic shifts that have altered the media industry during the last decade. Boidman explores how digital and mobile media are propelling the next wave of media, communications and advertising.

accomplishment_honors_awards.title: Forty Under 40
accomplishment_honors_awards.description:
http://www.digitalsignageconnection.com/pjsc-managing-director-mark-boidman-named-40-under-forty”-986

Peter J. Solomon Company (PJSC), a private investment banking firm, is pleased to announce that managing director Mark Boidman has been recognized as a 2015 40 Under Forty honoree by the National Association of Certified Valuators and Analysts (NACVA) and the Consultants'​ Training Institute (CTI).

The 40 Under Forty awards were created to recognize “the next generation of industry mavericks” across all spectrums of the financial consulting profession. The honorees were selected from a pool of more than 125 nominees and chosen by the executive staff of NACVA and the CTI as having made extraordinary advances in their fields.

Boidman joined PJSC in 2013 and is a managing director of the firm’s Media, Entertainment, Communications and Technology (MECT) Group, which focuses on digital media, marketing services and mobile and out-of-home media.

“Mark’s inclusion in ‘40 Under Forty’ is a tribute to his talent as well as his commitment to delivering high- quality advisory services,” PJSC founder and chairman Peter Solomon said.

“Our media team is at the forefront of developments in digital media, retail tech and location-based entertainment,” Rich Brail, managing director and head of PJSC’s MECT Group, said. “We bring this knowledge and a deep understanding of our clients' needs to each assignment. We congratulate Mark on his achievement.”

Boidman sits on the Board of Directors for the Digital Signage Federation (DSF), a not-for-profit trade organization serving the worldwide digital signage, interactive technologies and digital-out-of-home network industries.
